Title: Lock evidence-status vocabulary
Description: Audit every current claim and bind it to verified, preregistered, planned, or literature-reported evidence.
Acceptance: Evidence matrix contains no unscoped factual result and every verified numeric claim names a repository artifact.
Notes: Verified scope now distinguishes the bounded run from three unpooled seed-17/target-0.258 selected-weight scalability-smoke jobs.

Title: Verify closest-work bibliography
Description: Verify paper titles, author lists, years, BibTeX, official repositories, training lanes, and payload assumptions from primary sources.
Acceptance: At least twelve baseline records have primary-source metadata and each selected comparison has an explicit lane and fairness policy.
Notes: The 27-row paper subset now includes Harma, OBR, Hessian ECSQ, Optimal Formats, and ProjQ; literature numbers remain outside the measured leaderboard.

Title: Formalize theory obligations
Description: Derive sufficient exact-rate combination conditions with first-order, Hessian self/cross, and discrete byte terms.
Acceptance: Derivation defines assumptions, handles nonzero first-order loss, and identifies at least one failure condition matching the strict current result.
Notes: The sufficient theoretical mechanism is formalized; the empirical frontier claim and ablation remain planned.

Title: Freeze method component interfaces
Description: Audit the candidate, allocator, codec, and endpoint interfaces against notes/design/method-components.csv.
Acceptance: Every component has typed input/output, a replacement control, and a bounded novelty note.
Notes: Interfaces and replacement controls are recorded; novelty remains bounded by the primary-source audit.

Title: Repair allocator and protocol validity
Description: Replace the forced per-layer strict candidate with a nested global exact-feasibility frontier; route comfort selection to validation; disclose the whitened-SVD fit metric.
Acceptance: Code includes Q/Q+S_OBS/Q+L degenerate states, intermediate support refinements, exact final serialization, strict OBS and S/L order factors; protocol tests keep comfort off test; whitening floor and PSD minima are audited.
Notes: Implementation and static checks are complete locally; numerical execution and full test suite remain blocked by unavailable 210/network dependencies.

Title: Revalidate bounded physical-rate result
Description: Recompute summaries and confirm file length, SHA256, decode, formal completion, and endpoint metrics for the completed run.
Acceptance: All summary checks pass and the strict QL/QSL artifacts remain byte-identical in length with the recorded endpoint metrics.
Notes: This is validation of an existing artifact, not a new seed.

Title: Execute preregistered eight-seed confirmation
Description: Run all native endpoints under the fixed WikiText-2 manifest for eight seeds with source snapshots.
Acceptance: Eight complete runs and an aggregate file report per-seed values, mean, standard deviation, paired signs, and exact bytes.
Notes: The split is preregistered; model executions are not complete.

Title: Scale across model sizes and families
Description: Run the native endpoint matrix on larger Pythia models and a Qwen-family transfer set under explicit tensor scopes.
Acceptance: Each declared model has completed artifacts, disclosed seeds, identical within-model tensor scope, and exact-byte comparisons.
Notes: The completed three-job seed-17/target-0.258 selected-weight smoke does not satisfy this multi-seed, multi-rate, full-scope confirmatory issue.

Title: Validate three-job scalability smoke
Description: Validate the Pythia-70M full-MLP, OPT-125M five-depth MLP, and Qwen3-0.6B five-depth up/down-MLP jobs and build fail-closed within-model aggregates.
Acceptance: All three jobs are completed_valid; every selected-weight artifact size/hash, endpoint, fixed-window pair, Hessian decomposition, and 13-point path passes the aggregate checks, with no cross-model pooling.
Notes: One seed (17), one target selected-weight artifact ratio (0.258), 1,016 held-out tokens, and eight descriptive windows per job; not whole-model or confirmatory evidence.

Title: Execute 3B/7B method-and-scale smoke
Description: Run 15 declared jobs covering matched global/local-guard and full/randomized-SVD controls, OBS/order/support/low-rank/covariance factors, and depth-stratified Qwen2.5-3B, Llama2-7B, and Mistral-7B scopes.
Acceptance: Every job is completed_valid with live disk/GPU/model checks, exact artifact hashes, and no unplanned tensor-scope change; negative and degenerate allocator selections remain visible.
Notes: Externally blocked as of 2026-07-16: four ssh-dev Profile 210 attempts timed out and the workstation has no active route to 172.25.5.210; no remote writes or experiments occurred.

Title: Sweep exact rates and allocation granularity
Description: Measure strict exact-byte Pareto curves and test sparse-group, rank, and scale-block granularity.
Acceptance: Every plotted point round-trips, matches its byte budget, and has endpoint NLL plus provenance metadata.
Notes: Logical-value curves may appear only as labelled diagnostics.

Title: Run A0 and A1 direct baselines
Description: Evaluate no-backward RTN, quantization, pruning, and hybrid controls under matched calibration, scope, rate, and evaluation.
Acceptance: Selected A0/A1 baselines have protocol-matched outputs or are marked unavailable with a documented reason.
Notes: No B or C method enters this table.

Title: Run backward-assisted B track
Description: Evaluate local backward-assisted methods in a separate track with calibration tokens, steps, and updated state disclosed.
Acceptance: Each B-track row reports backward budget, exported payload, exact bytes, and endpoint metrics without comparison-language implying no-backward parity.
Notes: Includes methods such as SqueezeLLM, OmniQuant, SliderQuant, and LiftQuant block correction when runnable.

Title: Audit global-recovery C track
Description: Separate global task-recovery and QAT methods and record their optimization data, compute, and exported state.
Acceptance: C-track entries are either protocol-matched reproductions or clearly labelled literature-reported context with no direct frozen-PTQ ranking.
Notes: LiftQuant E2E remains blocked in the pinned audit until its entrypoint is repaired and verified.

Title: Complete component and geometry ablations
Description: Ablate OBS values, scale granularity, low-rank allocation, physical-rate charging, orthogonality terms, and endpoint validation.
Acceptance: Every high-priority component has a removal or replacement control and all outcomes include exact bytes and endpoint loss.
Notes: Negative ablations remain in the report.

Title: Measure resources and deployment gap
Description: Measure encoder time, peak memory, decode time, artifact bytes, and the gap between the research codec and any deployment format.
Acceptance: Resource metrics identify hardware, software version, repetitions, tensor scope, and whether the format is research-only or deployable.
Notes: Do not claim inference speedup from file size alone.

Title: Introduction
Description: Frame exact serialized rate, state the bounded strict negative result, and introduce the allocation question.
Acceptance: The section gives tensor and seed scope beside every current number and states the performance contribution as planned.
Notes: Target approximately 0.70 main-text page.

Title: Related work
Description: Position quantization, pruning, low-rank correction, and hybrids by training dependence and payload.
Acceptance: The section cites primary sources, separates training lanes, and does not reproduce unverified numeric leaderboards.
Notes: Keep the section technical rather than chronological.

Title: Problem formulation and exact rate
Description: Define rate scope, research codec, perturbations, task loss, Hessian metric, and exact-byte objective.
Acceptance: All symbols and charged payload fields are defined and the deployment-format limitation is explicit.
Notes: Target approximately 0.85 main-text page.

Title: Comfort-zone allocation method
Description: Describe candidate repairs, marginal gain per byte, discrete budget allocation, and endpoint acceptance.
Acceptance: The algorithm charges natural and aligned bytes, identifies every updated state, and does not assert unmeasured superiority.
Notes: Target approximately 1.35 main-text pages.

Title: Theory and diagnostics
Description: Present the perturbation expansion, correlation conditions, comfort-zone assumptions, endpoint limitation, and the boundary of the repeated smoke diagnostic.
Visualization: Figure 3 Hessian geometry and loss landscape
Acceptance: Every proposition states assumptions and verified diagnostics remain scoped to the bounded run or three separate smoke jobs.
Notes: Target approximately 1.15 main-text pages; the unexecuted full ablation remains E8.

Title: Experimental protocol
Description: Specify models, data, splits, seeds, tensor coverage, rates, baselines, metrics, provenance, and which stages are executed versus planned.
Acceptance: The protocol distinguishes verified smoke, preregistered, planned, and literature-only rows and defines every comparison lane.
Notes: Target approximately 0.75 main-text page; writing the protocol does not imply E2-E9 executed.

Title: Verified bounded results
Section_Path: Results and Analysis > Verified Bounded Evidence
Description: Report exact-byte endpoints, paired diagnostics, parameter efficiency, geometry, and loss landscapes.
Acceptance: Every value is recomputed from source files and captions state one seed, six tensors, and diagnostic statistical scope.
Notes: Lead with the negative strict equal-rate outcome.

Title: Confirmatory scale and frontier results
Section_Path: Results and Analysis > Multi-Seed and Scale
Description: Populate multi-seed model-scale, rate, and external-comparison results only after confirmatory executions complete.
Visualization: Exact-rate Pareto and confirmatory model-scale panels
Acceptance: No factual confirmatory result sentence or filled numeric cell exists without a completed output artifact and aggregation check.
Notes: The verified three-job smoke is tracked separately in W8a and does not complete this issue.

Title: Three scalability-smoke observations
Section_Path: Results and Analysis > Three Scalability-Smoke Observations
Description: Report the three within-model endpoint/geometry observations, parameter-utilization controls, and loss paths with explicit evidence boundaries.
Visualization: Compact smoke endpoint table plus efficiency/geometry and 13-point path panels
Acceptance: Prose and captions state seed 17, one selected-weight rate, exact tensor scopes, no pooling/significance/model-size trend, and keep C8/C12 planned.
Notes: The strict-scaled-QSL-minus-QL NLL sign is positive in all three separate jobs; this is a repeated diagnostic, not confirmatory inference.

Title: Limitations and conclusion
Description: Close with the bounded and unpooled-smoke takeaway, source-provenance boundary, deployment gap, and remaining confirmatory claims.
Acceptance: The conclusion contains no universal orthogonality, pooled/model-scale, production speed, or frontier-superiority claim unless the corresponding evidence is complete.
Notes: Target approximately 0.40 main-text page; planned W8 is not a prerequisite for a limitation-aware conclusion.

Title: Exact-byte problem schematic
Description: Create a structural diagram contrasting logical payload accounting with exact serialized bytes.
Visualization: Vector problem schematic with charged payload fields
Acceptance: The vector figure contains no empirical bar height and matches the method component inventory.
Notes: Structural figure.

Title: Verified geometry and landscape
Description: Generate publication panels directly from current geometry and landscape result files.
Acceptance: The plotting script records source paths, uses no title inside panels, and labels the bounded one-seed scope in the caption data.
Notes: No smoothing or invented uncertainty bands.

Title: Exact-rate Pareto curves
Description: Generate multi-seed Pareto panels only after rate sweeps complete.
Acceptance: Each point maps to a validated artifact and uncertainty is aggregated across disclosed seeds.
Notes: Do not generate from expected outcomes.

Title: Training-lane taxonomy
Description: Create a compact table of A0/A1/B/C training signal, updated state, payload, and comparison policy.
Acceptance: Every row is backed by a primary source or the audited repository matrix and no performance ranking appears.
Notes: Literature-reported protocol evidence only.

Title: Three-job scalability-smoke panels
Section_Path: Results and Analysis > Three Scalability-Smoke Observations
Description: Generate deterministic efficiency/geometry and loss-path panels from the fail-closed aggregate manifest.
Visualization: Vector/PDF and PNG efficiency-geometry plus six-path-per-job landscape panels
Acceptance: Plot manifests hash every input/output; labels enforce within-model reading, one seed/rate, descriptive windows, and Taylor-fit versus extrapolation boundaries.
Notes: No cross-model connecting line, pooled statistic, significance marker, or whole-model compression label.

Title: Apply paper rhythm refinement
Description: Refine sentence rhythm and claim calibration after all executed-evidence sections stabilize.
Acceptance: The revision preserves citations, equations, numeric values, evidence labels, and section intent.
Notes: Planned W8 remains out of the refinement dependency until confirmatory evidence exists.

Title: Validate contracts and evidence links
Description: Run issue/design validators and check every evidence path and status transition.
Acceptance: Validators pass and every verified claim resolves to an existing artifact.
Notes: Repeat after any paper-direction change.

Title: Compile and inspect ICML manuscript
Description: Compile the manuscript and scan for LaTeX errors, undefined citations/references, and overfull boxes.
Acceptance: The review PDF compiles with no LaTeX error, undefined citation/reference, or material overfull box.
Notes: Planned Pareto visual V3 is excluded until its required experiments exist; ICML style files must be source-controlled or provenance-documented.

Title: Independent claim and result review
Description: Ask an independent reviewer to audit claim scope, numeric backfill, comparison fairness, and negative-result visibility.
Acceptance: All blocking findings are fixed or recorded as explicit limitations before synchronization.
Notes: Required before final Git push.
